stimulus.content: On April 19, 1775, British soldiers marched to Lexington and Concord in Massachusetts. Colonial militia met them on the town green. Shots were fired, and the fighting marked the start of the American Revolution. These battles are known as 'the shot heard round the world.'

stem: Why are the battles at Lexington and Concord important in American history?

choices.text: They ended the French and Indian War
They led to the signing of a peace treaty
They forced Britain to lower all taxes

answer_explanation: The passage states that the fighting at Lexington and Concord marked the start of the American Revolution. The other choices describe events that did not result from these battles.
